subject: Re: WarpTools support for EER files?
body:
Absolutely
I put the list in CC so that others are aware of this too
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Aug 8, 2024, at 23:34, Valerie Perez <omegaphilter@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿Thank you very much for the quick reply!
I have another quick question: if our EER files are saved as gain-corrected, can I simply not specify a gain ref path to continue with the processing?
I do have the gain reference, but I do not have the un-corrected files.
Please let me know.
On Thursday, August 8, 2024 at 4:09:43 PM UTC-5 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
Hi Valerie,
General - Warp, M and friends
warpem.github.io
You need to add a few flags when creating your frame series settings file. 
Good luck!
Alister
 
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Aug 8, 2024, at 22:59, Valerie Perez <
omegap...@gmail.com
> wrote:
﻿Hello Warp Team,
I was wondering how I can utilize WarpTools to process EER data files for a tomography dataset. 
I am trying to follow the tutorial on the github page for processing frame stacks, but I'm not sure where I would implement the EER flag/info that is suggested in the directions for Warp (not WarpTools) at 
http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=1606
Can you please provide some guidance? I am very excited to use the Warp/M pipeline for this project!
Thank you,
Valerie
